Package org.eventb.core.ast

Class FormulaFactory

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.FormulaFactory
public class FormulaFactory extends Object
This class is the factory class for all the AST nodes of an event-B formula.

Use this class to instantiate all types of AST nodes used in an event-B formula. Tags used in this method are the constants defined in class Formula.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static boolean
    checkSymbol(String symbol)
    Deprecated.
    use isValidExtensionSymbol(String) instead
    static IExpressionExtension
    getCond()
    Returns the conditional expression extension.
    static FormulaFactory
    getDefault()
    Returns the default instance of the formula factory which corresponds to the current version of the mathematical language.
    IFormulaExtension
    getExtension(int tag)
    Returns the formula extension corresponding to the given tag, or null if the given extension is not supported by this factory.
    Set<IFormulaExtension>
    getExtensions()
    Returns all the extensions of the current formula factory.
    AbstractGrammar
    getGrammar()
    FIXME should either not be published, or return an interface
    IGrammar
    getGrammarView()
    Returns a view of the underlying grammar associated to this factory, through its operator groups.
    static FormulaFactory
    getInstance(Set<IFormulaExtension> extensions)
    Returns an instance of a formula factory supporting the given extensions (and only them) in addition to the regular event-B mathematical language.
    static FormulaFactory
    getInstance(IFormulaExtension... extensions)
    Returns an instance of a formula factory supporting the given extensions (and only them) in addition to the regular event-B mathematical language.
    static int
    getTag(IFormulaExtension extension)
    Returns the integer tag corresponding to the given formula extension, or NO_TAG if the given extension is unknown to the AST library (i.e., has never been used for any factory).
    static FormulaFactory
    getV1Default()
    Returns the default instance of the formula factory for the obsolete version V1 of the mathematical language.
    boolean
    hasExtension(int tag)
    Tells whether the given tag corresponds to a formula extension supported by this factory.
    boolean
    hasExtension(IFormulaExtension extension)
    Tells whether the given formula extension is supported by this factory.
    static boolean
    isEventBWhiteSpace(char c)
    Returns whether the given char is a white space according to the Event-B lexical specification.
    static boolean
    isEventBWhiteSpace(int codePoint)
    Returns whether the given code point is a white space according to the Event-B lexical specification.
    boolean
    isValidExtensionId(String id)
    Returns whether the given id is valid for an extension.
    boolean
    isValidExtensionSymbol(String symbol)
    Returns whether the given symbol is valid for an extension.
    boolean
    isValidIdentifierName(String name)
    Returns whether the given name is a valid identifier name (that is an identifier name which is not used as a keyword in event-B concrete syntax).
    boolean
    isValidPredicateName(String name)
    Returns whether the given name is a valid predicate variable name (that is any identifier name prefixed by a $).

    toString()
     
    IUpgradeResult<Assignment>
    upgradeAssignment(String input)
    Upgrades the given assignment string from language version V1 to the V2 using the current factory.
    IUpgradeResult<Expression>
    upgradeExpression(String input)
    Upgrades the given expression string from language version V1 to the V2 using the current factory.
    IUpgradeResult<Predicate>
    upgradePredicate(String input)
    Upgrades the given predicate string from language version V1 to the V2 using the current factory.
    FormulaFactory
    withExtensions(Set<IFormulaExtension> addedExtns)
    Returns a new formula factory that supports the extensions of this factory, together with the given extensions.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • getDefault

      public static FormulaFactory getDefault()
      Returns the default instance of the formula factory which corresponds to the current version of the mathematical language.
      Returns:
      the default instance of this class for language V2

    • getV1Default

      public static FormulaFactory getV1Default()
      Returns the default instance of the formula factory for the obsolete version V1 of the mathematical language.
      Returns:
      the single instance of this class for language V1
      Since:
      2.7

    • getInstance

      public static FormulaFactory getInstance(Set<IFormulaExtension> extensions)
      Returns an instance of a formula factory supporting the given extensions (and only them) in addition to the regular event-B mathematical language. The given set of extensions must contain all extensions of any datatype present, including the datatype dependencies (i.e., other datatypes directly or indirectly referenced in the datatype definitions).
      Parameters:
      extensions - set of mathematical extensions to support
      Returns:
      a formula factory supporting the given extensions
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given set of extensions is not datatype complete
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the given extensions is a type constructor that does not fulfill its contract
      Since:
      2.0, 3.0
      See Also:
      • IExpressionExtension.isATypeConstructor()

    • getInstance

      public static FormulaFactory getInstance(IFormulaExtension... extensions)
      Returns an instance of a formula factory supporting the given extensions (and only them) in addition to the regular event-B mathematical language. The given set of extensions must contain all extensions of any datatype present, including the datatype dependencies (i.e., other datatypes directly or indirectly referenced in the datatype definitions).
      Parameters:
      extensions - the mathematical extensions to support
      Returns:
      a formula factory supporting the given extensions
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given set of extensions is not datatype complete
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the given extensions is a type constructor that does not fulfill its contract
      Since:
      2.3, 3.0
      See Also:
      • IExpressionExtension.isATypeConstructor()

    • checkSymbol

      @Deprecated(since="3.8") public static boolean checkSymbol(String symbol)
      Deprecated.
      use isValidExtensionSymbol(String) instead
      Checks that a symbol is appropriate for the generic scanner. Such a symbol must either be an identifier, or must be not empty and purely symbolic (i.e., not contain any character allowable in an identifier). Identifier symbols with a prime are rejected by this method. Note that the symbol is tested with the generic scanner. This method does not check if the symbol is used by the extensions of a particular factory. To check this, use isValidExtensionSymbol(String).
      Parameters:
      symbol - symbol to test
      Returns:
      true iff the given symbol is valid
      Since:
      2.0

    • withExtensions

      public FormulaFactory withExtensions(Set<IFormulaExtension> addedExtns)
      Returns a new formula factory that supports the extensions of this factory, together with the given extensions.

      Adding already supported extensions has no effect. The resulting supported extensions are a union of known extensions and given extensions.

      The resulting set of extensions must contain all extensions of any datatype present, including the datatype dependencies (i.e., other datatypes directly or indirectly referenced in the datatype definitions).

      Parameters:
      addedExtns - a set of extensions to add
      Returns:
      a new factory supporting additional extensions
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the resulting set of extensions is not datatype complete
      Since:
      2.0, 3.0

    • makeDatatypeBuilder

      public IDatatypeBuilder makeDatatypeBuilder(String name, List<GivenType> parameters)
      Returns a new datatype builder for the datatype with the given name and type parameters. The type parameters are specified as given types.
      Parameters:
      name - the datatype name
      parameters - the formal type parameters of the datatype
      Returns:
      a datatype builder
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the datatype name is not a valid identifier in this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the type parameters names are in conflict with each other or with the datatype name
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the type parameters was built with another factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeDatatypeBuilder

      public IDatatypeBuilder makeDatatypeBuilder(String name, GivenType... parameters)
      Returns a new datatype builder for the datatype with the given name and type parameters. The type parameters are specified as given types.
      Parameters:
      name - the datatype name
      parameters - the formal type parameters of the datatype
      Returns:
      a datatype builder
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the datatype name is not a valid identifier in this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the type parameters names are in conflict with each other or with the datatype name
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the type parameters was built with another factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeDatatypeBuilder

      public IDatatypeBuilder makeDatatypeBuilder(String name, List<GivenType> parameters, Object origin)
      Returns a new datatype builder for the datatype with the given name and type parameters. The type parameters are specified as given types. The given origin object will be returned by IDatatype.getOrigin() in the finalized datatype.
      Parameters:
      name - the datatype name
      parameters - the formal type parameters of the datatype
      origin - the origin of the datatype, or null
      Returns:
      a datatype builder
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the datatype name is not a valid identifier in this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the type parameters names are in conflict with each other or with the datatype name
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the type parameters was built with another factory
      Since:
      3.1

    • getGrammar

      public AbstractGrammar getGrammar()
      FIXME should either not be published, or return an interface
      Since:
      2.0

    • getGrammarView

      public IGrammar getGrammarView()
      Returns a view of the underlying grammar associated to this factory, through its operator groups.
      Returns:
      a list of operator groups
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • IOperatorGroup

    • makeExtendedExpression

      public ExtendedExpression makeExtendedExpression(IExpressionExtension extension, Expression[] expressions, Predicate[] predicates, SourceLocation location, Type type)
      Returns a new extended expression.
      Parameters:
      extension - the expression extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      type - the type of the extended expression or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type is not valid
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type or some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

    • makeExtendedExpression

      public ExtendedExpression makeExtendedExpression(IExpressionExtension extension, Expression[] expressions, Predicate[] predicates, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new extended expression.
      Parameters:
      extension - the expression extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

    • makeExtendedExpression

      public ExtendedExpression makeExtendedExpression(IExpressionExtension extension, Collection<Expression> expressions, Collection<Predicate> predicates, SourceLocation location, Type type)
      Returns a new extended expression.
      Parameters:
      extension - the expression extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      type - the type of the extended expression or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type is not valid
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type or some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

    • makeExtendedExpression

      public ExtendedExpression makeExtendedExpression(IExpressionExtension extension, Collection<Expression> expressions, Collection<Predicate> predicates, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new extended expression.
      Parameters:
      extension - the expression extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

      2.0

      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

      public ExtendedPredicate makeExtendedPredicate(IPredicateExtension2 extension, Expression[] expressions, Predicate[] predicates, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new extended predicate.
      Parameters:
      extension - the predicate extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      3.6

      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

      Returns a new extended predicate.
      Parameters:
      extension - the predicate extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

    • makeExtendedPredicate

      public ExtendedPredicate makeExtendedPredicate(IPredicateExtension2 extension, Collection<Expression> expressions, Collection<Predicate> predicates, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new extended predicate.
      Parameters:
      extension - the predicate extension
      expressions - the children expressions
      predicates - the children predicates
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new extended expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the extension is not supported by this factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the preconditions of the extension on children are not verified
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      3.6
      See Also:
      • IExtensionKind.checkPreconditions(Expression[], Predicate[])

    • getCond

      public static IExpressionExtension getCond()
      Returns the conditional expression extension. This extension takes one predicate and two expression children. Its value is the first expression if the predicate holds, the second expression otherwise.
      Returns:
      the expression extension of the COND operator
      Since:
      2.0

    • getTag

      public static int getTag(IFormulaExtension extension)
      Returns the integer tag corresponding to the given formula extension, or NO_TAG if the given extension is unknown to the AST library (i.e., has never been used for any factory).
      Returns:
      the tag associated to the given extension or Formula.NO_TAG if unknown
      Since:
      2.0

    • getExtension

      public IFormulaExtension getExtension(int tag)
      Returns the formula extension corresponding to the given tag, or null if the given extension is not supported by this factory.
      Returns:
      the extension associated to the given tag or null if unsupported
      Since:
      2.0

    • hasExtension

      public boolean hasExtension(int tag)
      Tells whether the given tag corresponds to a formula extension supported by this factory. Consequently, returns false if the given tag does not correspond to a formula extension.

      This is a short-hand method fully equivalent to 

       factory.getExtension(tag) != null

      Returns:
      true iff the given tag corresponds to a formula extension supported by this factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • hasExtension

      public boolean hasExtension(IFormulaExtension extension)
      Tells whether the given formula extension is supported by this factory.
      Returns:
      true iff the given formula extension is supported by this factory
      Since:
      3.0

    • getExtensions

      public Set<IFormulaExtension> getExtensions()
      Returns all the extensions of the current formula factory.
      Returns:
      all extensions supported by this factory
      Since:
      2.0

    • makeAssociativeExpression

      public AssociativeExpression makeAssociativeExpression(int tag, Expression[] children, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new associative expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.BUNION set union
      • Formula.BINTER set intersection
      • Formula.BCOMP backward composition of relations
      • Formula.FCOMP forward composition of relations
      • Formula.OVR functional overriding
      • Formula.PLUS addition
      • Formula.MUL multiplication
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the associative expression
      children - the children of the associative expression (at least two)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new associative expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid associative expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the children collection contains less than two elements
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeAssociativeExpression

      public AssociativeExpression makeAssociativeExpression(int tag, Collection<Expression> children, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new associative expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.BUNION set union
      • Formula.BINTER set intersection
      • Formula.BCOMP backward composition of relations
      • Formula.FCOMP forward composition of relations
      • Formula.OVR functional overriding
      • Formula.PLUS addition
      • Formula.MUL multiplication
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the associative expression
      children - the children of the associative expression (at least two)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new associative expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid associative expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the children collection contains less than two elements
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeAssociativePredicate

      public AssociativePredicate makeAssociativePredicate(int tag, Collection<Predicate> predicates, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new associative predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.LAND conjunction
      • Formula.LOR disjunction
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the associative predicate
      predicates - the children of the associative predicate (at least two)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new associative predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid associative predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the children collection contains less than two elements
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeAssociativePredicate

      public AssociativePredicate makeAssociativePredicate(int tag, Predicate[] predicates, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new associative predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.LAND conjunction
      • Formula.LOR disjunction
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the associative predicate
      predicates - the children of the associative predicate (at least two)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new associative predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid associative predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the children collection contains less than two elements
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeAtomicExpression

      public AtomicExpression makeAtomicExpression(int tag, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new atomic expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.INTEGER the set of integers
      • Formula.NATURAL the set of natural numbers
      • Formula.NATURAL1 the set of non-null natural numbers
      • Formula.BOOL the set of Boolean values
      • Formula.TRUE the Boolean value TRUE
      • Formula.FALSE the Boolean value FALSE
      • Formula.EMPTYSET the empty set
      • Formula.KPRED the predecessor relation on integers
      • Formula.KSUCC the successor relation on integers
      • Formula.KPRJ1_GEN the generic first projection
      • Formula.KPRJ2_GEN the generic second projection
      • Formula.KID_GEN the generic identity relation

      The last three tags are not supported by a V1 factory.

      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the atomic expression
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new atomic expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid atomic expression tag

    • makeAtomicExpression

      public AtomicExpression makeAtomicExpression(int tag, SourceLocation location, Type type)
      Returns a new atomic expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.INTEGER the set of integers
      • Formula.NATURAL the set of natural numbers
      • Formula.NATURAL1 the set of non-null natural numbers
      • Formula.BOOL the set of Boolean values
      • Formula.TRUE the Boolean value TRUE
      • Formula.FALSE the Boolean value FALSE
      • Formula.EMPTYSET the empty set
      • Formula.KPRED the predecessor relation on integers
      • Formula.KSUCC the successor relation on integers
      • Formula.KPRJ1_GEN the generic first projection
      • Formula.KPRJ2_GEN the generic second projection
      • Formula.KID_GEN the generic identity relation

      The allowed type patterns are given below for each tag:

      • INTEGER, NATURAL, NATURAL1: ℙ(ℤ)
      • BOOL: ℙ(BOOL)
      • TRUE, FALSE: BOOL
      • EMPTYSET: ℙ(alpha)
      • KPRED, KSUCC: ℙ(ℤ)
      • KPRJ1_GEN: ℙ(alpha × beta × alpha)
      • KPRJ2_GEN: ℙ(alpha × beta × beta)
      • KID_GEN: ℙ(alpha × alpha)

      The last three tags are not supported by a V1 factory.

      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the atomic expression
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new atomic expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid atomic expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type is not valid
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      1.0

    • makeEmptySet

      public AtomicExpression makeEmptySet(Type type, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new empty set expression.
      Parameters:
      type - the type of the empty set. Must be null or a power set type
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new empty set expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type is not valid
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesEqualTo

      public BecomesEqualTo makeBecomesEqualTo(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes equal to" assignment.
      Parameters:
      ident - identifier which is assigned to (left-hand side)
      value - after-value for this identifier (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes equal to" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesEqualTo

      public BecomesEqualTo makeBecomesEqualTo(FreeIdentifier[] idents, Expression[] values, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes equal to" assignment.
      Parameters:
      idents - the identifiers which are assigned to (left-hand side)
      values - the after-values for these identifiers (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes equal to" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no assigned identifier
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of identifiers and after-values are not equal
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesEqualTo

      public BecomesEqualTo makeBecomesEqualTo(Collection<FreeIdentifier> idents, Collection<Expression> values, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes equal to" assignment.
      Parameters:
      idents - the identifiers which are assigned to (left-hand side)
      values - the after-values for these identifiers (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes equal to" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no assigned identifier
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of identifiers and after-values are not equal
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesMemberOf

      public BecomesMemberOf makeBecomesMemberOf(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression setExpr, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes member of" assignment.
      Parameters:
      ident - identifier which is assigned to (left-hand side)
      setExpr - set to which the after-value belongs (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes member of" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesSuchThat

      public BecomesSuchThat makeBecomesSuchThat(FreeIdentifier ident, BoundIdentDecl primedIdent, Predicate condition, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes such that" assignment.
      Parameters:
      ident - identifier which is assigned to (left-hand side)
      primedIdent - bound identifier declaration for primed identifier (after values)
      condition - condition on before and after values of this identifier (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes such that" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesSuchThat

      public BecomesSuchThat makeBecomesSuchThat(FreeIdentifier[] idents, BoundIdentDecl[] primedIdents, Predicate condition, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes such that" assignment.
      Parameters:
      idents - identifiers that are assigned to (left-hand side)
      primedIdents - bound identifier declarations for primed identifiers (after values)
      condition - condition on before and after values of these identifiers (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes such that" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no assigned identifier
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of identifiers and primed identifiers are not equal
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBecomesSuchThat

      public BecomesSuchThat makeBecomesSuchThat(Collection<FreeIdentifier> idents, Collection<BoundIdentDecl> primedIdents, Predicate condition, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "becomes such that" assignment.
      Parameters:
      idents - identifiers that are assigned to (left-hand side)
      primedIdents - bound identifier declarations for primed identifiers (after values)
      condition - condition on before and after values of these identifiers (right-hand side)
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new "becomes such that" assignment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no assigned identifier
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of identifiers and primed identifiers are not equal
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBinaryExpression

      public BinaryExpression makeBinaryExpression(int tag, Expression left, Expression right, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new binary expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.MAPSTO the maplet operator
      • Formula.REL the set of all relations
      • Formula.TREL the set of all total relations
      • Formula.SREL the set of all surjective relations
      • Formula.STREL the set of all total and surjective relations
      • Formula.PFUN the set of all partial functions
      • Formula.TFUN the set of all total functions
      • Formula.PINJ the set of all partial injections
      • Formula.TINJ the set of all total injections
      • Formula.PSUR the set of all partial surjections
      • Formula.TSUR the set of all total surjections
      • Formula.TBIJ the set of all total bijections
      • Formula.SETMINUS set subtraction
      • Formula.CPROD Cartesian product
      • Formula.DPROD direct product
      • Formula.PPROD parallel product
      • Formula.DOMRES domain restriction
      • Formula.DOMSUB domain subtraction
      • Formula.RANRES range restriction
      • Formula.RANSUB range subtraction
      • Formula.UPTO integer interval
      • Formula.MINUS integer subtraction
      • Formula.DIV integer division
      • Formula.MOD integer modulo
      • Formula.EXPN integer exponentiation
      • Formula.FUNIMAGE functional image
      • Formula.RELIMAGE relational image
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the binary expression
      left - the left-hand child of the binary expression
      right - the right-hand child of the binary expression
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new binary expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid binary expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBinaryPredicate

      public BinaryPredicate makeBinaryPredicate(int tag, Predicate left, Predicate right, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new binary predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.LIMP logical implication
      • Formula.LEQV logical equivalence
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the binary predicate
      left - the left-hand child of the binary predicate
      right - the right-hand child of the binary predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new binary predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid binary predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBoolExpression

      public BoolExpression makeBoolExpression(Predicate child, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new "bool" expression. Its tag will be Formula.KBOOL.
      Parameters:
      child - the predicate of the bool expression
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new bool expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeBoundIdentDecl

      public BoundIdentDecl makeBoundIdentDecl(String name, SourceLocation location)
      Creates a new node representing a declaration of a bound identifier. Its tag will be Formula.BOUND_IDENT_DECL.

      As the given name is just for convenience (it is not really part of the formula), it is only required to be valid in the default factory. This means that a reserved name introduced by a mathematical extension is accepted and will be replaced by an arbitrary identifier name when converting to String form.

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the identifier
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a bound identifier declaration
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is not a valid identifier name in the default factory
      See Also:
      • isValidIdentifierName(String)
      • makeFreeIdentifier(String, SourceLocation)
      • makeBoundIdentifier(int, SourceLocation)

    • makeBoundIdentDecl

      public BoundIdentDecl makeBoundIdentDecl(String name, SourceLocation location, Type type)
      Creates a new node representing a declaration of a bound identifier. Its tag will be Formula.BOUND_IDENT_DECL.

      As the given name is just for convenience (it is not really part of the formula), it is only required to be valid in the default factory. This means that a reserved name introduced by a mathematical extension is accepted and will be replaced by an arbitrary identifier name when converting to String form.

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the identifier
      location - the source location or null
      type - the type of the identifier or null
      Returns:
      a bound identifier declaration
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is not a valid identifier name in the default factory
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different factory
      See Also:
      • isValidIdentifierName(String)
      • makeFreeIdentifier(String, SourceLocation)
      • makeBoundIdentifier(int, SourceLocation)

    • makeBoundIdentifier

      public BoundIdentifier makeBoundIdentifier(int index, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new bound occurrence of an identifier. Its tag will be Formula.BOUND_IDENT.
      Parameters:
      index - the index in De Bruijn notation. Must be non-negative
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a bound identifier occurrence
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the index is negative
      See Also:
      • makeBoundIdentDecl(String, SourceLocation)
      • makeFreeIdentifier(String, SourceLocation)

    • makeBoundIdentifier

      public BoundIdentifier makeBoundIdentifier(int index, SourceLocation location, Type type)
      Returns a new bound occurrence of an identifier. Its tag will be Formula.BOUND_IDENT.
      Parameters:
      index - the index in the De Bruijn notation. Must be non-negative
      location - the source location or null
      type - the type of this identifier or null
      Returns:
      a bound identifier occurrence
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the index is negative
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different factory
      See Also:
      • makeBoundIdentDecl(String, SourceLocation)
      • makeFreeIdentifier(String, SourceLocation)

    • makeFreeIdentifier

      public FreeIdentifier makeFreeIdentifier(String name, SourceLocation location)
      Creates a new node representing a free occurrence of an identifier. Its tag will be Formula.FREE_IDENT.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the identifier
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a free identifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is not a valid identifier name
      See Also:
      • isValidIdentifierName(String)
      • makeBoundIdentDecl(String, SourceLocation)
      • makeBoundIdentifier(int, SourceLocation)

    • makeFreeIdentifier

      public FreeIdentifier makeFreeIdentifier(String name, SourceLocation location, Type type)
      Creates a new node representing a free occurrence of an identifier. Its tag will be Formula.FREE_IDENT.

      The name of the identifier must not be used to denote a given type in the type parameter, except when the identifier denotes a given set. In other words, the only case where the given name can occur in the type parameter is when the type is exactly ℙ(name).

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the identifier
      location - the source location or null
      type - the type of this identifier or null
      Returns:
      a free identifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is not a valid identifier name
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type is not valid
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different factory
      See Also:
      • isValidIdentifierName(String)
      • makeBoundIdentDecl(String, SourceLocation)
      • makeBoundIdentifier(int, SourceLocation)

    • makeIntegerLiteral

      public IntegerLiteral makeIntegerLiteral(BigInteger literal, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new integer literal. Its tag will be Formula.INTLIT.
      Parameters:
      literal - the integer value for this literal
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new integer literal

    • makeLiteralPredicate

      public LiteralPredicate makeLiteralPredicate(int tag, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new literal predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.BTRUE truth
      • Formula.BFALSE falsity
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new literal predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a literal predicate tag

    • makePredicateVariable

      public PredicateVariable makePredicateVariable(String name, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new PredicateVariable. Its tag will be Formula.PREDICATE_VARIABLE.

      Predicate variables are named with a special prefix ( PredicateVariable.LEADING_SYMBOL) followed by an identifier name. As predicate variables live in a scope different from regular identifiers, their suffix can be an otherwise reserved name (e.g., the name $id is valid).

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the predicate variable
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new predicate variable
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name does not start with PredicateVariable.LEADING_SYMBOL
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name without the PredicateVariable.LEADING_SYMBOL prefix is not a valid identifier
      Since:
      1.2
      See Also:
      • isValidPredicateName(String)

    • makeQuantifiedExpression

      public QuantifiedExpression makeQuantifiedExpression(int tag, BoundIdentDecl[] boundIdentifiers, Predicate pred, Expression expr, SourceLocation location, QuantifiedExpression.Form form)
      Returns a new quantified expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.QUNION quantified set union
      • Formula.QINTER quantified set intersection
      • Formula.CSET set comprehension

      One can also specify the written form of the expression. The allowed values are (in decreasing order of generality)

      • QuantifiedExpression.Form.Explicit explicit declaration of quantifiers
      • QuantifiedExpression.Form.Implicit implicit declaration of quantifiers
      • QuantifiedExpression.Form.Lambda lambda abstraction
      Lambda abstraction is only meaningful for set comprehension. If the given form is not compatible with the structure of the quantified expression, it will automatically be upgraded it to the least general that fits.

      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the quantified expression
      boundIdentifiers - the bound identifier declarations
      pred - the child predicate
      expr - the child expression
      location - the source location or null
      form - the written form of the quantified expression
      Returns:
      a new quantified expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a quantified expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no bound identifier declaration
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeQuantifiedExpression

      public QuantifiedExpression makeQuantifiedExpression(int tag, Collection<BoundIdentDecl> boundIdentifiers, Predicate pred, Expression expr, SourceLocation location, QuantifiedExpression.Form form)
      Returns a new quantified expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.QUNION quantified set union
      • Formula.QINTER quantified set intersection
      • Formula.CSET set comprehension

      One can also specify the written form of the expression. The allowed values are (in decreasing order of generality)

      • QuantifiedExpression.Form.Explicit explicit declaration of quantifiers
      • QuantifiedExpression.Form.Implicit implicit declaration of quantifiers
      • QuantifiedExpression.Form.Lambda lambda abstraction
      Lambda abstraction is only meaningful for set comprehension. If the given form is not compatible with the structure of the quantified expression, it will automatically be upgraded it to the least general that fits.

      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the quantified expression
      boundIdentifiers - the bound identifier declarations
      pred - the child predicate
      expr - the child expression
      location - the source location or null
      form - the written form of the quantified expression
      Returns:
      a new quantified expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a quantified expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no bound identifier declaration
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeQuantifiedPredicate

      public QuantifiedPredicate makeQuantifiedPredicate(int tag, BoundIdentDecl[] boundIdentifiers, Predicate pred, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new quantified predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.FORALL universal quantification
      • Formula.EXISTS existential quantification
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the quantified predicate
      boundIdentifiers - the bound identifier declarations
      pred - the corresponding predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new quantified predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a quantified predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no bound identifier declaration
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeQuantifiedPredicate

      public QuantifiedPredicate makeQuantifiedPredicate(int tag, Collection<BoundIdentDecl> boundIdentifiers, Predicate pred, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new quantified predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.FORALL universal quantification
      • Formula.EXISTS existential quantification
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the quantified predicate
      boundIdentifiers - the bound identifier declarations
      pred - the corresponding predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new quantified predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a quantified predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no bound identifier declaration
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeRelationalPredicate

      public RelationalPredicate makeRelationalPredicate(int tag, Expression left, Expression right, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new relational predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.EQUAL equality
      • Formula.NOTEQUAL disequality
      • Formula.LT less than
      • Formula.LE less than or equal to
      • Formula.GT greater than
      • Formula.GE greater than or equal to
      • Formula.IN membership
      • Formula.NOTIN non membership
      • Formula.SUBSET strict subset
      • Formula.NOTSUBSET not strict subset
      • Formula.SUBSETEQ subset or equal to
      • Formula.NOTSUBSETEQ neither subset nor equal to
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the relational predicate
      left - the left-hand child of the relational predicate
      right - the right-hand child of the relational predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new relational predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a valid relational predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeSetExtension

      public SetExtension makeSetExtension(Expression expression, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new singleton set. Its tag will be Formula.SETEXT.
      Parameters:
      expression - the unique member of this singleton set
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new set extension
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given member has been built with a different factory

    • makeSetExtension

      public SetExtension makeSetExtension(Expression[] members, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new set extension. Its tag will be Formula.SETEXT.
      Parameters:
      members - the members of the set extension
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new set extension
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given member has been built with a different factory

    • makeEmptySetExtension

      public SetExtension makeEmptySetExtension(Type type, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new set extension containing no child, but having the given type. Its tag will be Formula.SETEXT.

      This method is hardly useful to end-users, except when writing tests. One should prefer using makeEmptySet(Type, SourceLocation).

      Parameters:
      type - the type of the set extension
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new empty set extension of the given type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type is not a powerset type
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      2.6

    • makeSetExtension

      public SetExtension makeSetExtension(Collection<Expression> members, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new set extension. Its tag will be Formula.SETEXT.
      Parameters:
      members - the members of the set extension
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new set extension
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given member has been built with a different factory

    • makeSimplePredicate

      public SimplePredicate makeSimplePredicate(int tag, Expression child, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new simple predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.KFINITE finiteness
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the simple predicate
      child - the child of the simple predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new simple predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a simple predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeTypeEnvironment

      public ITypeEnvironmentBuilder makeTypeEnvironment()
      Returns a new empty type environment.
      Returns:
      a new empty type environment
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeUnaryExpression

      public UnaryExpression makeUnaryExpression(int tag, Expression child, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new unary expression. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.KCARD set cardinality
      • Formula.POW powerset
      • Formula.POW1 powerset without empty set
      • Formula.KUNION generalized union
      • Formula.KINTER generalized intersection
      • Formula.KDOM domain
      • Formula.KRAN range
      • Formula.KMIN minimum
      • Formula.KMAX maximum
      • Formula.CONVERSE converse of a relation
      • Formula.UNMINUS opposite

      For backward compatibility with the mathematical language V1, the following additional tags are also accepted by a V1 factory

      • Formula.KPRJ1 unary first projection
      • Formula.KPRJ2 unary second projection
      • Formula.KID unary identity
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the unary expression
      child - the child of the unary expression
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new unary expression
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a unary expression tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeUnaryPredicate

      public UnaryPredicate makeUnaryPredicate(int tag, Predicate child, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new unary predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.NOT logical negation
      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the unary predicate
      child - the child of the unary predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new unary predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a unary predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given child has been built with a different factory

    • makeMultiplePredicate

      public MultiplePredicate makeMultiplePredicate(int tag, Expression[] children, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new multiple predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.KPARTITION partition

      This method is not supported by a V1 factory.

      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the multiple predicate
      children - the children of the multiple predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new multiple predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a multiple predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no child
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      1.0

    • makeMultiplePredicate

      public MultiplePredicate makeMultiplePredicate(int tag, Collection<Expression> children, SourceLocation location)
      Returns a new multiple predicate. The allowed tag values are
      • Formula.KPARTITION partition

      This method is not supported by a V1 factory.

      Parameters:
      tag - the tag of the multiple predicate
      children - the children of the multiple predicate
      location - the source location or null
      Returns:
      a new multiple predicate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the tag is not a multiple predicate tag
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no child
      IllegalArgumentException - if some given child has been built with a different factory
      Since:
      1.0

    • parseAssignment

      public IParseResult parseAssignment(String formula, Object origin)
      Parses the specified formula and returns the corresponding result. This method does not allow predicate variables.
      Parameters:
      formula - the formula to be parsed
      origin - the origin to be traced to the built AST
      Returns:
      the result of the parse
      Since:
      3.0

    • parseExpression

      public IParseResult parseExpression(String formula, Object origin)
      Parses the specified formula and returns the corresponding result. This method does not allow predicate variables.
      Parameters:
      formula - the formula to be parsed
      origin - the origin to be traced to the built AST
      Returns:
      the result of the parse
      Since:
      3.0

    • parseExpressionPattern

      public IParseResult parseExpressionPattern(String formula, Object origin)
      Parses the specified formula and returns the corresponding result. This method allows predicate variables.
      Parameters:
      formula - the formula to be parsed
      origin - the origin to be traced to the built AST
      Returns:
      the result of the parse
      Since:
      3.0

    • parsePredicate

      public IParseResult parsePredicate(String formula, Object origin)
      Parses the specified predicate and returns the corresponding result. This method does not allow predicate variables.
      Parameters:
      formula - the formula to be parsed
      origin - the origin to be traced to the built AST
      Returns:
      the result of the parse
      Since:
      3.0

    • parsePredicatePattern

      public IParseResult parsePredicatePattern(String formula, Object origin)
      Parses the specified predicate and returns the corresponding result. This method allows predicate variables.
      Parameters:
      formula - the formula to be parsed
      origin - the origin to be traced to the built AST
      Returns:
      the result of the parse
      Since:
      3.0

    • parseType

      public IParseResult parseType(String formula)
      Parses the specified type and returns the corresponding result. This method does not allow predicate variables.
      Parameters:
      formula - the formula to be parsed
      Returns:
      the result of the parse
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeBooleanType

      public BooleanType makeBooleanType()
      Returns the type which corresponds to the set of booleans.
      Returns:
      the predefined boolean type

    • makeParametricType

      public ParametricType makeParametricType(IExpressionExtension typeConstructor, List<Type> typePrms)
      Returns the instance of the parametric type of the given constructor with the given type parameters.
      Parameters:
      typeConstructor - the constructor of the parametric type
      typePrms - the parameters of the parametric type
      Returns:
      a new parametric type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given extension is not supported by this factory or is not a type constructor
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of type parameters does not correspond to the type constructor specification
      IllegalArgumentException - if some type parameter has been built with a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • IExpressionExtension.isATypeConstructor()
      • IExtensionKind.checkTypePreconditions(Type[])

    • makeParametricType

      public ParametricType makeParametricType(IExpressionExtension typeConstructor, Type... typePrms)
      Returns the instance of the parametric type of the given constructor with the given type parameters.
      Parameters:
      typeConstructor - the constructor of the parametric type
      typePrms - the parameters of the parametric type
      Returns:
      a new parametric type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given extension is not supported by this factory or is not a type constructor
      IllegalArgumentException - if the number of type parameters does not correspond to the type constructor specification
      IllegalArgumentException - if some type parameter has been built with a different formula factory
      Since:
      3.0
      See Also:
      • IExpressionExtension.isATypeConstructor()
      • IExtensionKind.checkTypePreconditions(Type[])

    • makeGivenType

      public GivenType makeGivenType(String name)
      Returns the type which corresponds to the carrier-set with the given name.
      Parameters:
      name - name of the type
      Returns:
      a given type with the given name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is not a valid identifier name
      See Also:
      • isValidIdentifierName(String)

    • makeGivenType

      public GivenType makeGivenType(FreeIdentifier freeIdentifier)
      Returns the type which corresponds to the carrier-set named after the given free identifier.

      The free identifier argument must be typed as a valid given type, i.e its type must be a powerset over a given type of its name. That is, for an identifier named 'S', its type must be POW(S).

      Calling GivenType.toExpression() on the returned type will return the given free identifier. Thus, in particular, the source location (if any) is retained.

      Parameters:
      freeIdentifier - a free identifier typed as a given set
      Returns:
      a new given type with given identifier name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given free identifier is not typed as a given set.
      Since:
      3.0

    • makeIntegerType

      public IntegerType makeIntegerType()
      Returns the type which corresponds to the set of all integers.
      Returns:
      the predefined integer type

    • makePosition

      public static IPosition makePosition(String image)
      Returns the formula position corresponding to the given string image.
      Parameters:
      image - string image of the position, should have been obtained from IPosition.toString().
      Returns:
      the position denoted by the given string
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given string doesn't denote a formula position

    • makePowerSetType

      public PowerSetType makePowerSetType(Type base)
      Returns the type corresponding to the power set of the given type.
      Parameters:
      base - the base type to build upon
      Returns:
      the power set type of the given type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type has been built with a different formula factory

    • makeProductType

      public ProductType makeProductType(Type left, Type right)
      Returns the type corresponding to the Cartesian product of the two given types.
      Parameters:
      left - the first component of the Cartesian product
      right - the second component of the Cartesian product
      Returns:
      the product type of the two given types
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the the given types has been built with a different formula factory

    • makeRelationalType

      public PowerSetType makeRelationalType(Type left, Type right)
      Returns the type corresponding to the set of all relations between the two given types.

      This is a handy method fully equivalent to the call 

       makePowerSetType(makeProductType(left, right));

      Parameters:
      left - the domain of the relations
      right - the range of the relations
      Returns:
      the relational type between the two given types
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if one of the the given types has been built with a different formula factory

    • makeSpecialization

      public ISpecialization makeSpecialization()
      Creates a new empty specialization.
      Returns:
      a fresh empty specialization
      Since:
      2.6

    • isValidExtensionSymbol

      public boolean isValidExtensionSymbol(String symbol)
      Returns whether the given symbol is valid for an extension. This will check that the symbol is an identifier or is purely symbolic and that it does not conflict with existing extensions. Note that, contrary to isValidIdentifierName(String), this rejects identifiers with a prime.
      Parameters:
      symbol - symbol to test
      Returns:
      whether the given symbol is valid or not
      Since:
      3.8

    • isValidExtensionId

      public boolean isValidExtensionId(String id)
      Returns whether the given id is valid for an extension. This will check that the id does not conflict with existing extensions.
      Parameters:
      id - id to test
      Returns:
      whether the given id is valid or not
      Since:
      3.8

    • isValidIdentifierName

      public boolean isValidIdentifierName(String name)
      Returns whether the given name is a valid identifier name (that is an identifier name which is not used as a keyword in event-B concrete syntax). A name with a prime at the end is considered to be a valid identifier by this method. On the other hand, isValidExtensionSymbol(String) only accepts identifiers without a prime (but it also accepts purely symbolic strings).
      Parameters:
      name - the name to test
      Returns:
      true if the given name is a valid name for an identifier

    • isValidPredicateName

      public boolean isValidPredicateName(String name)
      Returns whether the given name is a valid predicate variable name (that is any identifier name prefixed by a $).
      Parameters:
      name - the name to test
      Returns:
      true if the given name is a valid name for a predicate variable
      Since:
      3.2

    • isEventBWhiteSpace

      public static boolean isEventBWhiteSpace(char c)
      Returns whether the given char is a white space according to the Event-B lexical specification.
      Parameters:
      c - the char to test
      Returns:
      true if the given char is a white space

    • isEventBWhiteSpace

      public static boolean isEventBWhiteSpace(int codePoint)
      Returns whether the given code point is a white space according to the Event-B lexical specification.
      Parameters:
      codePoint - the code point to test
      Returns:
      true if the given char is a white space
      Since:
      2.1

    • upgradeAssignment

      public IUpgradeResult<Assignment> upgradeAssignment(String input)
      Upgrades the given assignment string from language version V1 to the V2 using the current factory.
      Parameters:
      input - an assignment string assumed to be parsable in the language version V1
      Returns:
      the result of the upgrade
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the current factory is a V1 version
      Since:
      3.0

    • upgradeExpression

      public IUpgradeResult<Expression> upgradeExpression(String input)
      Upgrades the given expression string from language version V1 to the V2 using the current factory.
      Parameters:
      input - an expression string assumed to be parsable in the language version immediately preceding the target version
      Returns:
      the result of the upgrade
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the current factory is a V1 version
      Since:
      3.0

    • upgradePredicate

      public IUpgradeResult<Predicate> upgradePredicate(String input)
      Upgrades the given predicate string from language version V1 to the V2 using the current factory.
      Parameters:
      input - a predicate string assumed to be parsable in the language version immediately preceding the target version
      Returns:
      the result of the upgrade
      Since:
      3.0

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
